skill.name: review-architecture
skill.description: Review backend and frontend architecture against project coding rules, design documents, and CI review standards.
skill.summary: Checks architecture, code conventions, documentation alignment, and review readiness for project changes.
skill.author: Crearize

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are Markdown code spans, document file names, and fenced examples rather than executable code. The skill is low risk because it legitimately asks the agent to read repository files and optionally use git history for architecture review.

low_findings.title: Repository File Access Required for Review
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to use Read, Grep, and Glob to inspect project documents and changed files. This is expected for architecture review, but it requires access to repository contents.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly lists Read, Grep, and Glob as required tools and names project documents to inspect. The access is purposeful and scoped to review work, so the risk is minor.

low_findings.title: Optional Git Command Use
low_findings.description: The skill recommends Bash for git log review. This is a legitimate code review task, but command execution should remain limited to read-only repository inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only clear shell-related instruction is a read-only git history check. No evidence found of arbitrary command construction, persistence, or destructive commands.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command alerts correspond to Markdown code spans, file paths, and fenced report templates. They are not Ruby or shell execution in the skill source.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows Markdown documentation and examples rather than executable source code. No evidence found of runtime backtick execution or command substitution.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match documentation names and review text, including md file references and error code examples. No cryptographic algorithm use is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation references such as database-design.md and error-codes.md. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic implementation.

content.value_statement: Architecture reviews often miss project-specific rules and documentation drift. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured backend, frontend, and documentation checks.
actual_capabilities: Checks backend package structure, naming, service design, DTOs, MyBatis usage, Flyway changes, and OpenAPI updates.
Checks frontend TypeScript, React Hooks, Next.js App Router, shadcn/ui usage, component structure, forms, and performance.
Compares implementation changes with project coding rules, architecture documents, feature specifications, and API documentation.
Separates required fixes from medium priority recommendations in review reports.
Asks reviewers to avoid repeating issues that were already resolved in earlier review comments.
limitations: Designed for the MovieMarketer project structure and document names.
Does not run automated tests or static analyzers by itself.
Requires access to the repository documents and changed files for useful results.
May need adaptation before use with different Java, TypeScript, or Next.js conventions.

use_cases.title: Pre-merge Architecture Review
use_cases.description: Review a pull request for backend, frontend, database, and API design issues before merge.

use_cases.title: Feature Documentation Check
use_cases.description: Confirm that feature specifications, database design, error codes, and API documents match code changes.

use_cases.title: Project Rule Compliance Review
use_cases.description: Check whether new code follows local Java, TypeScript, React, and shadcn/ui conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small change
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current changes with the architecture review skill. Focus on required fixes first, then list optional improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Check Documentation Alignment
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether this change updates the required architecture, API, database, and error code documents. Report missing updates with file references.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Backend and Frontend Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the backend and frontend changes against project coding rules. Separate blocking violations from medium priority recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Against CI Review Standards
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a strict architecture review equal to the project CI review standard. Include pass or fail judgment and exact remediation steps.

output_examples.input: Review a pull request that changes a service, DTO, and API document.
output: Overall judgment with pass or fail status.
Backend findings grouped by package structure, service design, DTO design, and documentation alignment.
Required fixes with file references and concrete remediation guidance.

output_examples.input: Check a frontend feature that adds a page, form, and presentational component.
output: Frontend findings covering TypeScript strictness, React Hooks, App Router placement, and shadcn/ui usage.
Clear distinction between required fixes and optional suggestions.
Next step recommendation for the developer or reviewer.

best_practices: Read the listed project documents before judging implementation quality.
Ask for changed files or pull request context before starting a full review.
Keep findings specific, actionable, and tied to project rules.
anti_patterns: Using the skill without access to the relevant project documents.
Treating optional suggestions as blocking issues without rule evidence.
Applying MovieMarketer-specific conventions to unrelated projects without adaptation.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews architecture, coding rules, documentation alignment, and CI-style review readiness for backend and frontend changes.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it execute code?
faq.answer: No direct execution is defined. It recommends read and search tools, with optional git history inspection through Bash.

faq.question: Is it project-specific?
faq.answer: Yes. It references MovieMarketer documents, workflows, and coding conventions.

faq.question: Can it replace human review?
faq.answer: No. It structures review coverage, but a reviewer should verify context and final decisions.

faq.question: Why is the security risk low?
faq.answer: The static alerts were false positives. The remaining risk is normal repository file access for code review.
